Added a player class that has attributes of a playername and a postion

Split the player into a player data object and a player class. The Player class is the controller and the PlayerData class is the DAO. Also built out the Enemy class to be able to take damage, and reward xp to the players animentals on death.
Added enemy health and the ability for them to take damage and die. Taking damage does not account for elemental types at the moment.

Added the attribute group class which is a collection of attributes. Then changed Gems to be a subclass of the attribute group class. Doing this allows for more flexible future attribute groups such as weapons or armour.
Got the gem cost of power up water type animentals working. Need more tests of higher level animentals though
Fixed some bugs in the ExpNumber class. Then adjusted the tests and code to use ExpNumber for player.gold. Now I can get almost infinately large doubles with Math.Pow!
Got Divide, Subtract, and Pow working and tested.
Added improved gitignore to each project
Fixed 3 failing tests that dealth with multiplication where the current number gets smaller.
Added a bunch of adding tests and a bit more adding logic for ExpNumber. Then started working on mulitplication of ExpNumbers
Started working on implementing animental leveling up. Got leveling up through xp, and generating weighted rarity tiers working
Added functionality to merge 3 gems together to make a higher tier gem.
Added level up functionality to attributes

Added the Saphhire and some tests to go along with it. Changed the way Attributes apply thier values to make it less likely that I'll make an error. Removed some responsibility from the gem tests. Only the attribute tests should be testing the explicit values of the attribute at each stage.

Made it so that calling Apply() on an attribute over and over does not continually make the attribute stronger. Also added the topaz and started testing and coding it. Currently am in the process of writing tests that ensure that each gem is getting attributes of the correct type

Worked on adding a ruby, and making my tests and code more thourough for all gems. Examples of this include: making sure calling Apply() over and over again does not infinatly increase gem power, and giving attributes the ability to display thier value
Initial commit. I have some basic game logic working. Implemented the logic to create the emerald object and apply its attributes to the player. This also required created 3 Attribute classes.
Got the front end somewhat setup. Also got clicking logic working on mosters.
Got full scale integration test to work!
Initial commit. Script can handle find messages so far

Have things in a state that data collection is working well, and predictions are working with 50-60% accuracy. I tried bying a few 'underpriced' cats and sold them for what the algorithm thinks they're worth. We'll see how that goes.

Fixed some issues with items and their attributes. Then I got items to actually spawn and change color with their rarity. Then I got the temporary inventory that the player will use to keep or discard items they get during the level working. Now I just need to make the interface to keep or discard items, and make the inventory! Today was a very chill coding session. It was pretty great.
Finished the upgrading system for towers! Onto the item system nowgit add -A :/!
Got the tower purchase/sell ui working! Also improved the art for the fire bolt a little, but not much. Am quite tired now.
Got the tower purchase menu mostly implemented. Need to do a little bit of polish, and bug fixing but it's almost there.

Made the UI a bit prettier and started adding functionality to the tower purchasing menu. I don't like UI work very much... At least this style of ui work. It would probably be better with HTML and CSS.
Started building out the new UI. It is a vast improvement to the old one! It's going as slowly as I feared however. But hey, Gradatim Ferociter
Drew up some pretty buttons for the UI. Started programming the select tower ui element. It brings up the buttons currently. Now I just need to add functionality to those buttons.

Got sunrise and sunset working and tied it to the level progresion. Also got the last tower done! It's a fire bolt tower. It shoots a fire bolt that has a small explosive radius on hit. The radius grows to a maximum size of 4. The idea is that it does a lot of damage on a high cooldown.
Got flamethrower mostly done. Now am working on night and day.
Added a flak tower. it shoots air and stuff.
Started working on the shop ui and getting the camera to display pixel art well.
Got Darkness and lamps working pretty well. Just need to make it so that when the lampsa re removed, the darkenss is removed correclty. Also drew some pixel art I may or may not use.

Got lighting pretty much working! It was a lot of work. Now I need to make they lighting more dynamic so that lanterns can be built and the lighting changes.  I'm sorry if there's some bad code in this bunch. I'm tired :(
Got the wall breaker to a decent point. It can break walls and stuff. Also implemented health on walls and fixed some pathing bugs I think.
Got block deletion working in a efficient way!
Fixed pathing issues, Added wall costs and mining, Added health/damage system,
Initial commit. Have pathing of zombies functioning and wall building complete. Also have initial build of a turret working.
